heroText: Manama is a capital city in Bahrain, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

highlights.name: Manama
highlights.text: Manama is a capital city in Bahrain, connecting local landmarks, event context, transport access and seasonal reasons to plan a visit.

history.text: By 410–420 AD, a Nestorian bishopric and monastery was established in Al Dair, on the neighbouring island of Muharraq.
The yearly income of exported Bahraini pearls was 600,000 ducats, collected by around 2,000 pearling dhows.
In 1330, under the Jarwanid dynasty, the island became a tributary of the Kingdom of Hormuz.
In 1521, Bahrain fell to the expanding Portuguese Empire in the Persian Gulf, having already defeated Hormuz.
After numerous revolts and an expanding Safavid empire in Persia, the Portuguese were expelled from Bahrain and the Safavids took control in 1602.
Sometime after 1736, Nader Shah constructed a fort on the southern outskirts of Manama (likely the Diwan Fort).

With the ascension of Isa ibn Ali Al Khalifa as the Hakim of Bahrain in 1869, Manama became the centre of British activity in the Persian Gulf, though its interests were initially strictly commercial.
Trading recovered fully by 1873 and the country's earnings from pearl exports increased by sevenfold between 1873 and 1900.
Following the outbreak of World War I in 1914, the British Raj used Manama as a military base of operations during the Mesopotamian campaign.
In 1947, following the end of the war and subsequent Indian independence, the British residency of the Persian Gulf moved to Manama from Bushire.
The Dilmun civilisation inhabited the area in 3000 BC, serving as a key regional trading hub between Mesopotamia, Magan and the Indus Valley Civilisation.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Bahrain, particularly Manama and the nearby settlement of Bilad Al Qadeem, became a centre of Shia scholarship and training for the ulema, it would remain so for centuries.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The Portuguese consolidated their hold on the island by constructing the Bahrain Fort, on the outskirts of Manama.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The Safavids, sidelining Manama, designated the nearby town of Bilad Al Qadeem as the provincial capital.
